articles.title: The case for a new global framework to tackle environmental crime
articles.author: Diego Pérez Damasco
articles.summary: The postThe case for a new global framework to tackle environmental crimeappeared first onGlobal Initiative.
articles.analysis: GI-TOC propone un marco global contra el crimen ambiental pero no especifica mecanismos de enforcement efectivos frente a la fragmentación jurisdiccional actual.

articles.title: Criminal coexistence: The illicit ecosystem of the Southern Cone’s triple border
articles.author: Diego Pérez Damasco
articles.summary: The postCriminal coexistence: The illicit ecosystem of the Southern Cone’s triple borderappeared first onGlobal Initiative.
articles.analysis: El análisis del ecosistema criminal en la triple frontera del Cono Sur revela coexistencia entre grupos sin abordar por qué las estrategias de confrontación han fracasado.

articles.title: Playing legislative catch-up: Drug law reform in the Pacific
articles.author: Diego Pérez Damasco
articles.summary: The postPlaying legislative catch-up: Drug law reform in the Pacificappeared first onGlobal Initiative.
articles.analysis: La reforma legislativa en el Pacífico muestra el desfase entre marcos legales y realidades del mercado de drogas, pero omite evaluar impactos en reducción de daños.

articles.title: On the Radar: A Border Dispute Creates Criminal Opportunity
articles.author: InSight Crime
articles.summary: On the Radar: A Border Dispute Creates Criminal OpportunityThis week in On the Radar, a Colombia–Ecuador trade dispute fuels contraband at the border, Peru’s election highlights a state struggling to contain crime, and illegal mining tied to cocaine flows expands in Costa Rica.The postOn the Radar: A Border Dispute Creates Criminal Opportunityappeared first onInSight Crime.
articles.analysis: La disputa comercial Colombia-Ecuador genera oportunidades criminales que evidencian cómo tensiones estatales alimentan economías ilícitas transfronterizas.

articles.title: Arrest in Bolivia Signals Transnational Expansion of Colombian Gangs
articles.author: Sara Garcia
articles.summary: Arrest in Bolivia Signals Transnational Expansion of Colombian GangsThe arrest of a key criminal figure from Colombia’s Pacific coast in Santa Cruz, Bolivia, reveals that Colombian groups are expanding their presence across borders.  Jorge Isaac Campaz Jiménez, alias “Mapaya,” leader of the Espartanos, one of the most important criminal groups on Colombia’s Pacific coast, was arrested on April 6 b
articles.analysis: La detención en Bolivia confirma la expansión transnacional de grupos colombianos pero no examina las condiciones estructurales que facilitan su arraigo regional.

articles.title: Cocaine and Gold: The Battle to Protect Costa Rica’s Corcovado National Park
articles.author: Parker Asmann
articles.summary: Cocaine and Gold: The Battle to Protect Costa Rica’s Corcovado National ParkAs cocaine trafficking oozes into other criminal economies like illegal mining inside Costa Rica's national parks, it's become increasingly complicated for park rangers to protect its natural resources amid a lack of resources and operational support.The postCocaine and Gold: The Battle to Protect Costa Rica’s Corcovado Na
articles.analysis: La convergencia entre narcotráfico y minería ilegal en Costa Rica demuestra la evolución hacia economías criminales híbridas que desbordan capacidades estatales tradicionales.

articles.title: After El Mencho: A Data-Driven Look at an Evolving Drug Market
articles.author: Steven Dudley
articles.summary: After El Mencho: A Data-Driven Look at an Evolving Drug MarketThe story of El Mencho, it seemed to me, was the story of methamphetamine.At least that was my hypothesis, so I went about trying to prove it. But that was challenging. The information I needed to show how this had unfolded over a nearly 20-year period in which El Mencho operated is fragmented. And I needed different types of informatio
articles.analysis: El análisis post-El Mencho basado en datos revela la evolución del mercado de metanfetaminas pero no conecta patrones de producción con rutas hacia Europa.

articles.title: Europol-supported global operation targets over 75 000 users engaged in DDoS attacks
articles.summary: On 13 April 2026, 21 countries joined forces in a coordinated action week that focused on enforcement and prevention measures against over 75 000 criminal users engaging in distributed denial-of-service (DDoS)-for-hire services. With over 75 000 warning emails and letters being sent to identified criminal users and 4 arrests, the action week also led to the takedown of 53 domains  and the issuing
articles.analysis: La operación global contra ataques DDoS alcanza 75.000 usuarios pero privilegia medidas punitivas sobre estrategias preventivas estructurales en ciberseguridad.

articles.title: Leading Balkan Cartel member arrested in Montenegro
articles.summary: On 15 April 2026, Europol supported law enforcement authorities in Montenegro in conducting a large-scale operation targeting a significant cell of the Balkan Cartel. Europol also supported law enforcement authorities in Germany regarding the investigation targeting potential contact persons. This action day resulted in the arrest of twelve suspects (four in Germany), including a High Value Target
articles.analysis: La detención del Cartel de los Balcanes en Montenegro confirma su resiliencia organizativa pese a operaciones coordinadas que no alteran las estructuras de poder subyacentes.

articles.title: Spain: 24 arrested in crackdown on speedboat migrant smuggling network
articles.summary: The suspects, who are Algerian, Moroccan, and Spanish nationals, maintained the migrant smuggling network’s strong presence in Almería, Spain. While based primarily in this Spanish region, the criminal organisation is connected to other criminal groups and cells operating in France, particularly in Marseille and Perpignan, as well as in La Jonquera, Spain. These interconnections facilitated a robu
articles.analysis: La red de contrabando de migrantes en España evidencia la sofisticación de organizaciones transnacionales que Europol persigue sin abordar causas estructurales de la migración irregular.

articles.title: Frontex: Irregular border crossings down 40% in Q1 2026
articles.analysis: La reducción del 40% en cruces irregulares según Frontex no distingue entre efectividad de controles y redirección hacia rutas alternativas más peligrosas.

articles.title: The Return of Power Politics to the Market: Theory and Practice of the Geoeconomic Zeitenwende
articles.author: Hanns Günther Hilpert; Sascha Lohmann
articles.summary: The return of power politics to the market is a defining feature of the geoeconomic Zeitenwende, as is currently being experienced in inter­national politics. This has brought renewed attention to the long-standing conventional wisdom that economic activity can not only generate prosperity but also promote foreign and security policy objectives. The analysis and strategy of foreign, security, and
articles.analysis: SWP teoriza el retorno de la geopolítica al mercado pero no examina cómo esta dinámica afecta la cooperación europea en seguridad y políticas antidrogas.

articles.title: Iran greift kritische Infrastruktur in den USA an und ein neues KI-Modell sorgt für digitale...
articles.author: Alexandra Paulus
articles.summary: Warum reichen simple Cybersecurity-Basics aus, um kritische Systeme anzugreifen? In dieser Folge geht es um eine Warnung von US-Behörden vor iranischen Cyberangriffen auf Steuerungsanlagen kritischer Infrastruktur. Außerdem reden wir über das neue KI-Modell von Anthropic – und warum das jetzt Ängste vor dem Kollaps der Cybersicherheit schürt.
articles.analysis: Los ciberataques iraníes a infraestructura crítica estadounidense exponen vulnerabilidades que sistemas básicos de ciberseguridad deberían prevenir pero fallan sistemáticamente.

articles.title: Landelijke aansturing en coördinatie aanpak mensenhandel: Eindverslag van kennistafels
articles.author: Emma Zürcher
articles.summary: Study on national coordination of the Dutch approach to trafficking in human beings (THB), identifying governance bottlenecks and assessing the potential role of a National Coordinator in improving coherence, oversight and victim support.
articles.analysis: RAND identifica cuellos de botella en la coordinación holandesa contra trata de personas pero no evalúa si un coordinador nacional solucionaría problemas estructurales de governance.

articles.title: Korea Must Redefine Energy Security as a Geopolitical Imperative
articles.summary: A simulation of a blockade of the Taiwan Strait and South China Sea and the recent blockade of the Strait of Hormuz underscored that diversifying energy supply routes, maritime logistics, and data systems is no longer optional for Korea—it is a strategic necessity.
articles.analysis: La simulación de bloqueo del Estrecho de Taiwan revela vulnerabilidades energéticas coreanas que impactan cadenas de suministro globales incluyendo precursores químicos para drogas sintéticas.
